concept.title: API Testing Overview
concept.shortdesc: API testing in Postman verifies that an API behaves correctly by sending requests and validating responses against expected values. Tests are written in JavaScript and run automatically after each response.

conbody.p: API testing is the process of verifying that an API returns the correct responses for a given set of inputs. In Postman, you write tests as JavaScript assertions that run immediately after a response is received. Postman reports pass or fail results for each assertion in the Test Results tab.

section.title: Types of API Tests
section.p: Postman supports the following categories of API tests:

dlentry.dd: Verify that an API endpoint returns the correct data for a given request. For example, confirm that a GET request for a user returns the expected user object with the correct fields.
Confirm that the API returns the expected HTTP status code. For example, a successful POST request should return 201 Created.
Validate that the response body matches a predefined JSON schema, ensuring the API contract is maintained across releases.
Assert that the API responds within an acceptable time threshold, such as under 500 milliseconds.
Verify that the API returns appropriate error codes and messages for invalid inputs, missing fields, or unauthorized requests.

section.title: API Testing Workflow
section.p: A typical API testing workflow in Postman consists of the following steps:

ol.li: Create a collection to group related API requests.
Add requests to the collection for each API endpoint you want to test.
Configure environments and variables to manage base URLs and authentication tokens across environments.
Write test scripts in the Tests tab of each request.
Use the Collection Runner to execute all requests in sequence and review the test results.

section.title: Postman Test Framework
section.p: Postman uses the Chai assertion library through the pm.expect() API and the pm.test() function to define and run tests. Each test consists of a descriptive name and an assertion function.
Postman also provides a Snippets panel in the Tests tab that inserts commonly used test patterns with a single click. For a complete list of available snippets, see Test Snippets.
